commands.review_summary:
The PR makes implicit OpenAI-family image/PDF media defaults auth-mode-aware, substitutes Codex for eligible OAuth-only image defaults, drops unusable OAuth-only PDF OpenAI candidates, and passes agentDir into isolated Codex media startup.

PR surface: Source +319, Tests +488. Total +807 across 9 files.

Reproducibility: yes. from source inspection and linked logs: current main can select direct OpenAI media candidates from OAuth-visible provider auth while direct OpenAI media execution requires API-key auth; I did not run a live OAuth setup.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Protected auth-provider default-routing work needs maintainer review; the contributor should address the incomplete auto-candidate filtering and add live OAuth proof.

target.title: [codex] Fix OpenAI OAuth media routing
target.body:
## Summary

AI-assisted bug fix for #87168.

This PR makes implicit image/PDF media-understanding model selection auth-mode-aware for OpenAI-family defaults:

- keeps implicit `openai/...` media candidates only when direct OpenAI API-key-capable auth is available for the direct `openai-responses` route
- substitutes `codex/gpt-5.5` for implicit OpenAI image defaults only when canonical OpenAI OAuth plus the Codex app-server route are both available
- drops unusable implicit OpenAI PDF candidates under OAuth-only auth instead of substituting Codex, because PDF fallback execution currently uses generic `complete()` rather than the Codex media provider
- preserves explicit `openai/...` and `codex/...` overrides
- forwards `agentDir` into isolated Codex media app-server client startup so agent-scoped OAuth is available at execution

## Root Cause

The old selection path checked provider-level auth and treated canonical OpenAI OAuth as enough to select direct OpenAI media models. Runtime execution later requires API-key auth for `openai-responses`, producing the misleading `OPENAI_API_KEY` error. Codex image media can use the Codex app-server route, but that route must be selected deliberately and only when the auth signal is real.

## Verification

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/tools/model-config.helpers.test.ts src/agents/tools/image-tool.test.ts src/agents/tools/pdf-tool.model-config.test.ts src/media-understanding/defaults.test.ts src/agents/model-auth.profiles.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/codex/media-understanding-provider.test.ts`
- `git diff --check`
- `.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode local --stream-engine-output` clean: no accepted/actionable findings reported

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: OAuth-only OpenAI auth no longer selects unusable direct OpenAI media defaults; image auto-selection uses Codex when the Codex media route is actually available, while PDF drops the bad OpenAI slot.

Real environment tested: Local OpenClaw focused Vitest lanes in the isolated worktree.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: See the Verification section above.

Evidence after fix: Focused media/tool tests passed, Codex media provider test passed, `git diff --check` passed, and autoreview returned clean.

Observed result after fix: Regression coverage proves OAuth-only image defaults select `codex/gpt-5.5`, direct OpenAI API-key auth keeps `openai/...`, explicit overrides remain unchanged, legacy `openai-codex:*` profiles do not trigger Codex behavior, and PDF avoids reintroducing OpenAI fallback candidates under OAuth-only auth.

What was not tested: Live/Crabbox ChatGPT/Codex OAuth-only proof with no `OPENAI_API_KEY`; no such provisioned live environment was available in this session.

Fixes #87168
target.author: bek91
